entry.title: Implico's Strategic Approach to Bottled Gas
entry.content:
Background: An Introduction to the Bottled Gas Industry

In the oil and gas industry, the bottled gas process involves the production, storage, transportation, and distribution of gases stored in portable containers like cylinders or tanks, serving various industrial, commercial, and residential applications. 

Many companies operate diversified gas distribution units, engaging in both bulk and bottled gas distribution. This dual approach enables them to cater to a broader range of customers and markets, leveraging their infrastructure, logistics, and expertise in handling both forms of gas. 

Implementing a system that effectively manages both bulk and bottled gas processes is essential for these companies. By integrating such a system, businesses streamline operations and enhance user experience, facilitating efficient implementation and usage. 

 

Benefits of Bottled Gas Integration in S4SCSD Solution: 

With no other system integrated into S4SCSD, Implico's offering of bulk and bottled gas solutions presents a significant opportunity for market expansion and revenue growth.  

 

1. Comprehensive Solution  

By providing bulk and bottled gas solutions within one system, Implico offers a comprehensive portfolio that meets the diverse needs of gas distribution companies,  

 

2. Integrated Solutions 

Having bulk and bottled gas solutions readily available on S4SCSD opens doors to the market, as many companies operate in both segments. 

 

3. Cost-Effective Management 

Offering a unified system for managing bulk and bottled gas operations translates into financial benefits for customers, reducing implementation costs compared to integrating multiple systems. 

 

4. Exclusive Gas Solutions 

Implico's status as the sole provider of integrated bulk and bottled gas solutions within S4SCSD offers a unique opportunity to increase revenue streams and eliminate revenue sharing with other suppliers. 

 

5. Innovate, Enhance, Differentiate 

Investing in innovation and enhancing bulk and bottled gas solutions in S4SCSD will differentiate Implico in the market, meeting evolving customer needs and establishing itself as a reliable partner for gas distribution management. 

 

Workflow 

A general scenario has been developed, starting with the creation of the sales order, passing through the dispatching tool (IDM) to standard data reconciliation (PAT), considering the return of empty and/or damaged bottles, which is a common practice in this type of business. Thus, Implico's bottled gas scenario enhances the already existing SDM solution by ensuring that the entire downstream logistic process for bulk material and bottled gas is covered by one tool. 

Important Features within the Process: 

  • Delivery Trip Planning: Ability to plan the delivery trips based on the special characteristics of the truck, such as the number of cages used to organize the cylinders in the truck, etc. 
  • Return Process: Efficient handling of the return process for full, empty as well as defective bottles.
  • Bottle Management: Handling of different sizes of bottles and automatic empty bottles return to stock at the end of each trip. 

 

Final Implementation 

The bottled gas scenario is a new functionality within the SDM solution and will be fully integrated into the existing processes. This ensures that customers have both bulk and bottled gas business processes consolidated into one solution, providing a comprehensive overview. 

With this solution, we support the business model of a full energy service provider for fuels and gas. 

 

Conclusion 

In conclusion, the integration of bulk and bottled gas solutions into S4SCSD represents a strategic opportunity for Implico to expand its market presence, drive revenue growth, and solidify its position as a leading provider of integrated solutions in the oil and gas industry. 

Read more about Bottled Gas here: https://www.implico.com/bottled-gas/

Let's shape a sustainable future, together.

 

SAP PAT: Implico’s Data Collation, especially its Process Automation Toolset (PAT), has revolutionized the way energy companies manage their data reconciliation and settlement processes. Developed by Implico and natively integrated with SAP S4SCSD, PAT uses cutting-edge Robotic Process Automation (RPA) technology to automate repetitive and manual tasks across multiple business functions.

SAP IDM: Integrated Dispatch Management (IDM) by Implico is a robust solution designed to enhance these capabilities within SAP S4SCSD. This advanced functionality is tailored for industries such as oil, gas and chemicals, where efficient logistics and environmental compliance are of paramount importance.

 

SAP S/4HANA SDM: This tool offers a cohesive approach to the trade and transport of liquids and half-solids, integrating seamlessly with the SAP system to streamline processes from demand forecasting to invoicing, thereby facilitating smoother operations in hydrocarbon product trading.

SAP S/4HANA RFNO: A dedicated solution for managing service station networks, it caters to various service station types, offering functionalities like wet goods management and loyalty card management, aiming to enhance the fuel retail experience.

Implico’s Quality Assurance Program for SAP solutions: Implico wants to ensure the implementation of SAP SDM/RFNO solutions is professional and error-free – not only when doing so ourselves but also when external companies are contracted to oversee implementation. This is why we also offer two partner services that are designed to supplement the implementation projects based on a methodological approach and with highly skilled consultants, both of which help to achieve the defined targets: the Partner Governance Service and the Partner Advisor Service.

entry.title: Re: Dynamic programming fail: pass table TYPE cds as a parameter
entry.content:
Hello Ulrich,

the problem is in the compatibility and I hope it could be only due to incorrect syntax:
 "The type of "I_T_DATA_OBS" cannot be converted to the type of "ME->T_ITEMS""

In the calling program, I have the i_t_data parameter declared as: t_data_obs TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zfi_data_obs, which should do the job. I tried explicit typing, too, with no effect. ZFI_DATA_OBS is a CDS view entity.

The constructor is importing the parameter as: i_t_data_obs TYPE ANY TABLE.

The receiving object in the class's private attributes is declared as: t_items TYPE REF TO data (basically the only option, which does not trigger a syntax error).

Any ideas or recommendations, please?

Thank you.

Denis

entry.title: Re: Customized rating field in the performance form
entry.content:
Hello,

Whatever the final case, as design, the performance form will multiply the weight with goal rating. So you need to have a rating without weight multiplication. What you can do in that case is that you can have rating from manager and divide it by 100 in the goal plan xml, then have the actual-achievement as item rating/100. After that it will be automatically multiplied with item weight.

Have a nice day,

entry.title: (Re)Discover the Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators from SAP with our new Elearning
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 

Did you know that SAP’s Out-of-the-Box Business Key Figures, now referred to as Process Performance Indicators (PPIs), have been available for over 15 years? Since their introduction, these PPIs have become fundamental to a wide range of process performance analysis tools for SAP ECC and SAP S/4HANA. They played a critical role in tools such as the SAP Solution Manager-based SAP Business Process Improvement for SAP Solutions and Business Process Monitoring, and the SAP Innovation and Optimization tools. Now, it is also one of the key pillars of SAP Signavio Process Insights.

We are happy to share that we released a new e-learning course designed to guide you through the capabilities of SAP’s PPIs and the tools available within your SAP ES maintenance that will allow you to harness their potential to maximize your business’s efficiency.

Here’s what you can expect from the course: 

  1. Get an overview of SAP’s PPIs. Learn what Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators are, and understand how they provide immediate insights into business processes.
  2. Discover the powerful process analysis tools that come as part of your existing SAP maintenance contract and how you can leverage them to continuously optimize business processes.
  3. Learn how you can easily get started with the tools and apply the PPIs to your specific business process improvement goals.

Access the E-Learning:

Leveraging the Out-of-the-Box Process Performance Indicators to Improve Business Processes

Requirement: Registration for the SAP Learning Hub is required to utilize e-learning sessions.  If you are not currently registered, please do so here: SAP Enterprise Support Academy

Join us in the Value Maps: 

If you are not yet signed up for Value Maps, please join via this link.

You will discover new opportunities to understand and analyze the bottlenecks in business processes and improve the identified challenges. The Business Process Transformation value map helps you to innovate your business processes in an ever-changing world. The SAP ES Value Maps require no additional costs to access for our SAP Enterprise Support and SAP PSLE customers.

Please don't hesitate to get in touch with our team using the following form. (Please select the "SAP Signavio | Business Process Transformation" topic).

entry.title: Re: Dynamic programming fail: pass table TYPE cds as a parameter
entry.content:
You are right, Sandra. Frustrated in the middle of a night, I did not consider this.

I get error: "The type of "I_T_DATA_OBS" cannot be converted to the type of "ME->T_ITEMS" and "T_ITEMS is not an internal table".

I expect: pass atable  structure corresponding to the structure of a CDS view entity as a parameter, so that I can populate it with data of that CDS in the receiving class.

I tried: declaring t_data_obs in the calling program as TYPE STANDARD TABLE, as table, and explicitely. I tried, desperate, also this in the receiving class:

*  CREATE DATA t_items LIKE i_t_data_obs.
*  FIELD-SYMBOLS: <fs_items> TYPE STANDARD TABLE.
*  ASSIGN t_items->* TO <fs_items>.
*  <fs_items> = i_t_data_obs.

I assume that the problem lies in declaring the receiving structure as a reference to data in the private attributes:

t_items TYPE REF TO data,

and I hope someone can give me a recommendation for the correct declaration.

Regards,

Denis

entry.title: Does SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation require a special licenses or agreement?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have activated SAP Cloud ALM for monitoring (Operations). We would like to extend the same for Project creation and Transport management (Implementation). 

Is there any additional license required for Project configuration and Transport management system configuration. If license required, then what kind for license is require. Pls clarify.

 

Regards

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Does SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation require a speci...
entry.content:
Any Customer number that is entitled to an SAP Cloud ALM tenant is able to make use of its full suite of functionality without any additional requirements. This includes both the Operations functionality (monitoring, metrics, alerting, etc.) and the Implementation functionality (Project Management, Feature deployment, Testing, etc.). No additional licenses are required to make use of specific functionality of SAP Cloud ALM. 

Full steps to configure SAP Cloud ALM and its integrations can be found in the Setup and Administration for SAP Cloud ALM guide. More detailed information about how to actually use the functionality (split into different categories, such as Operations, Implementation, etc.) can be found in the SAP Cloud ALM Application Help.

entry.title: Re: BEHAVIOR IN STATUS PROFILES IN SALES VA02:
entry.content:
Hello @carlosgomezp 

You are right - my set up works unless you reach READ then go back to DRAF and want to go to CANC. Here the set up breaks. That is because the status management component "memorizes" the highest status. So if you reach READ, its status number 10 is memorized. So even if you get back from READ to DRAF, you can't go from DRAF to CANC, even if apparently the set up should allow that. 

That bug/feature is explained in the note 2047781 - BS042 or BS043 Error when status change is made. You can change that logic globally with modification note 159553 - Error messages BS042,BS043 or use the BAdI enhancement BSV_STATUS_PROFILE to implement your own validation logic. The BAdI is described in the note 1627673 - BAdI for status profile evaluation

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Benefit From SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02
entry.content:
Latest Innovations in a Nutshell

Continuing the blog series about updates on MDG innovations in SAP S/4HANA, I'd like to provide you with details on the latest innovations in SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 02. Highlights include:

  • Generative AI for assisted changes/summary of changes in central governance (classic mode) and for business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) using Joule AI copilot.
  • Improved data quality analytics for business partner and product master data.
  • Enrichment of business partner from CDQ reference data​ as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

These come in addition to various continuous improvements in central governance (classic mode).

Innovations in Detail

Let's take a closer look at the innovations.

Inclusion of Generative AI in MDG

Generative AI is used for summary of changes and assisted changes in central governance (classic mode), and for optional business partner processing (in cloud-ready mode) initiated by a Joule dialog. 

The AI-generated summary of changes provides you with an overview of changes in natural language (using a large language model) enabling you to: ​

  • verify the completeness and/or correctness of master data changes.​
  • get a brief overview of the changes comprised in an MDG change request.​
  • avoid pointless revisions based on incomplete changes.​
  • have a non-technical summary of changes (in contrast to change documents).​
  • facilitate approver’s final checks.

Generative AI-based summary of changes

​The AI-assisted change lets you change master data attributes with as little effort and as few steps as possible so that applying changes to a master data object is time efficient. You can make prompts in natural language to make field changes.

The AI-assisted change in central governance is applicable for business partner and material, and the AI-generated summary of changes in central governance is applicable for business partner, material, and custom objects which do not use editions.

In addition, SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA integrates with the AI copilot Joule, enabling queries in natural language and navigation to SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA cloud-ready mode for follow-up actions on business partner (incl. supplier and customer) data. (This innovation can be consumed as of FPS01).

Joule integration with follow-ups in MDG (cloud-ready mode)

These initial steps of including Generative AI in MDG processes are planned to be continued to cover more use cases in MDG classic mode and cloud-ready mode. 

"Data Quality Analytics 2.0" for business partner and product master data

  • SAP Analytics Cloud comes with an enhanced dashboard for chief data officers or master data specialists to get a real-time quality abstract on the prevailing quality and history trends of product and business partner master data, providing:
    • data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure by category, dimension, and rule
    • data quality analytics for seven business partner segments, e.g., Business Partner Core Data, Customer Sales Data, and Supplier Purchasing Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Country, Company Codes etc.
    • data quality analytics for seven product master data segments, e.g., Plant data, Sales Data, and Classification Data, each with drill-down using master data attributes or/and organization unit e.g., Product Group, Plant etc.
    • embedded authorization control via live data connection
    • integration in MDG system for data remediation

Data quality overview in 3-level reporting structure: by category, dimension, and ruleDeep-dive data quality analytics on business partner in SAP Analytics Cloud

  • Analytics CDS views for master data quality of business partner and product provide data source (CDS views) for master data quality analytics of business partner and product using organizational unit and attributes in SAP S/4HANA.

Enrichment of business partner records from reference data as an integrated part of consolidation, mass processing, etc.

In cloud-ready mode, the enrichment of business partner records from DDQ reference data is enabled as an integrated and automated step within:

  • Single Processing​
  • Mass Processing​
  • Consolidation​
  • Federation processes

Mass enrichment of business partners with reference data in SAP S/4HANA

For more information about the enrichment of business partner records from reference data with full process integration, read the SAP Community blog post by Martin Steinhauser.

Integration of SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

As an alternative to BTP Workflow Service (subscription required), the SAP Business Workflow can be assigned to process steps in the process template configuration. It can trigger any custom-defined action after a process step (e.g. email notification).

Integrate SAP Business Workflow as local workflow engine (cloud-ready mode)

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (material data)

Increased effectiveness through enhanced copy function in search and use of validation rules for classification in central governance for material: 

  • You can copy a selected material from the Search Materials Result List and choose one of three different copying options: Only Basic Data, All Data, or By Organizational Levels. When selecting Only Basic Data, basic data, long texts, and classification are copied. Choosing All Data will result in the complete data from the template material being copied. If you select By Organization Level, a dialog box will appear allowing you to choose which organizational data should be copied.​ 

Copy function in material search
Additionally, if a change request exists for the template material, the changed data from staging will be included in the copied material.

  • You can use validation rules for classes (table KSSK) and characteristics (table AUSP), defined using the rule management functionality of MDG, data quality management to check data during change request processing.

Validation rules for classes and characteristics

Continued improvements for central governance in classic mode (business partner incl. customer and supplier data)

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02 comes with improved Partner Functions content display and relationship categories in business partner processing:

  • The relationship type allows you to classify the relationship category into different subdivisions. Accordingly, you can maintain the relationship type on the user interface for Relationships, Contact Persons, Shareholder Relationships, and their related details user interface.
  • In addition, there are more search criteria when using the F4 Help on the Partner field in the partner functions lists ERP Customer Sales Areas: Partner Functions or ERP Vendor Purchasing Organization: Partner Functions. The F4 help also includes Business Partner, Customer, Supplier and Maximum No. of Hits as search criteria. The UIBBs for Partner Functions include the column Assigned BP. 

UIBB Partner Functions

Maintaining the Relation Type

As you can see from the outlined innovations, both classic mode in MDG and cloud-ready mode are continuously enhanced. The driver for innovations in classic mode is primarily the SAP Continuous Influence program, whereas cloud-ready mode in MDG is the strategic development path going forward.

To get the full picture of planned innovations in SAP Master Data Governance across the entire MDG solution portfolio up to 2025, see the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

To stay up to date, simply follow the SAP Community Topic Page for SAP Master Data Governance and join the Q&A and discussion forum.

entry.title: SAP S/4 Hana - Zero Downtime Option
entry.content:
I am working on Release Upgrade from S/4 Hana 2021 to SAP S/4 Hana 2023, I’ve been diving into the Zero Downtime Option (ZDO) and wanted to make it easier for everyone to understand this game-changing approach for software updates.

Why ZDO?

System downtime during updates can be costly and disruptive. Imagine upgrading your system without halting operations—ZDO makes that possible! It allows updates for SAP S/4 HANA without technical downtime by keeping users working seamlessly while the update happens in the background.

How ZDO Works?

  1. Up-time Processing starts with your original system (Release 1). Users work as usual.
  2. Once ZDO is initiated, users transition smoothly to a bridge subsystem, which mimics the source system while the update is prepared in the background.
  3. Transactional Consistency is maintained throughout the process, ensuring your data stays intact.
  4. The target release (Release 2) is then activated, with minimal disruption. After a short restart (just 5-15 minutes), users are moved to the new release without them even realising it.
    Key Benefit
  • Minimal downtime: A single restart, no database reboot.
  • Low resource footprint: Only necessary tables are cloned.
  • No extra licenses or tools required.

ZDO not only reduces downtime but also helps keep business operations flowing seamlessly, ensuring system availability while upgrades happen in parallel

#sap #sapcommunity #SAPS/4HanaUpgrades #S/4HANA2021TO2023 #S4Hana @#S/4Hana

entry.title: Re: Internal behaviour of dump marker and rsync marker in...
entry.content: Hi Dilip, The behavior to sync for MSA(using dump marker) and WSB is quite similar, ie there both exists enable marker. For MSA, an enable marker will first be sent out, following a dump marker. If you choose resync with "dump marker" for MSA, you have to purge the queue first. The existing data will still be there , and it will cause failure. That's also the benefit of using "resync marker" instead of "dump marker", as with resync marker, it will save the effort of manually cleaning up the queue. Hope this is clear now. Cheers Tony

entry.title: Re: Initial value of 0FISCPER is replaced wrongly
entry.content:
Hi Peter

0FISCPER is of type NUMC. The initial value for NUMC is 0. Since 0FISCPER is a NUMC with a length of 7, the initial value is 0000000.

BW did not allowed the usage of # on it's own as a characteristic value, as # represents the type corresponding initial value of a characteristic. So # is only used as a placeholder/mask to display the initial values in queries.

173241-Permitted characters in BW

===================================================

The following characteristic values can never be permitted:

  • The value that consists only of the single character '#' is not permitted because the initial input is masked with '#' for variables.
  • Values starting with the character '!' are not permitted because the variable entry is deleted at the first position due to this character.
  • Values that contain characters that contain the hexadecimal values HEX00 to HEX1F.

===================================================

entry.title: Error while testing SNMP TRAP from Solution Manager: "can't exec external program (2)" and "Exter
entry.content:
Introduction

Integrating SAP Solution Manager with ServiceNow via SNMP traps is a common practice to automate incident management and streamline IT operations. While testing SNMP traps, you might encounter the error messages "can't exec external program (2)" and "External program terminated with exit code 1."

Error Screenshot:

Troubleshooting Steps:

Make sure trapsend binary is in the $DIR_EXE directory of the instance and the <sidadm> user can start it

set the environment variable NLSUI_7BIT_FALLBACK to run the program

Execute the command ./trapsend -V

From the above screenshot we could understand the the Program is compiled with ICU version 65.1 of libicuu* library files. Current exe directory does not have the required version of the library files.

Solution:

Download the latest kernel file SAP KERNEL 7.93 64-BIT UNICODE PL 101 and copy the required libicuu* library files and place in the $DIR_EXE directory of the instance.

Required result:

Connection established

entry.title: Configure an SAP CAP Application to Connect to an SAP HANA Cloud Database in Another Global Account
entry.content:
How to Configure an SAP CAP Application to Connect to an SAP HANA Cloud Database in Another Global Account or Subaccount Without HDI

When your SAP HANA Cloud database is in another Global Account or subaccount, which prevents the natural use of the HDI container for database object management, you need to follow the steps below to configure your SAP CAP application and achieve a successful connection.

1: Configure a User-Provided Service

It is essential to create a user-provided service using the Cloud Foundry command line. This is important because SAP CAP requires the service to have three specific tags to automatically determine which configuration to use.

Run the following command:

cf create-user-provided-service ups-database-external -t "hana,database,relational"
  • ups-database-external: This is the name you assign to your service.
  • -t "hana,database,relational": These are the tags that CAP uses to identify the database service.

2: Modify the Service's JSON in SAP BTP

After creating the service via the command line, you need to specify the connection data in the service's JSON in SAP BTP.

The JSON should contain the following:

{
  "encrypt": true,
  "host": "your-hana-cloud-host",
  "password": "your-password",
  "port": "443",
  "schema": "YOUR_SCHEMA_NAME",
  "sslValidateCertificate": true,
  "user": "your-username"
}
  • host: The address of your SAP HANA Cloud instance.
  • user and password: The credentials of the user with permissions to access the database schema.
  • schema: The schema where your database objects are located or will be created.
  • encrypt and sslValidateCertificate: Settings to ensure the connection is secure via SSL/TLS.
  • port: The connection port, generally "443" for secure connections.

3: Modify package.json or .cdsrc.json

You need to tell SAP CAP which service to use to obtain the credentials. This is done by modifying your project's package.json or .cdsrc.json file.

Add or update the "requires" section as follows:

{
  "requires": {
    "db": {
      "kind": "hana",
      "credentials": {
        "service": "ups-database-external"
      }
    },
    "[production]": {
      "auth": "xsuaa",
      "db": {
        "kind": "hana",
        "credentials": {
          "service": "ups-database-external"
        }
      },
      "data": false
    }
  }
}
  • "service": "ups-database-external": Indicates to CAP that it should use the provided service named ups-database-external.
  • "[production]": Ensures that the same configuration is used in the production profile.

4: Remove HDI-Related Configurations

Since you are not using HDI for database object management, you need to remove all references and configurations related to HDI in your mta.yaml file or your project's deployment configurations.

This includes:

  • Removing modules or resources that create or manage HDI containers.
  • Deleting dependencies between modules and HDI resources.

5: Bind the Service to the CAP Application Instance in SAP BTP

For your CAP application to take the specified credentials and configuration, you need to bind the provided service to your application's instance in SAP BTP.

You can do this in two ways:

Option 1: Manual Binding

After deploying the application, run the command:

cf bind-service your-application-name ubs-database-external

Then, restart your application:

cf restart your-application-name

Option 2: Automatic Binding in mta.yaml

Add the binding in your mta.yaml file so that it is performed automatically during deployment.

Example of how to define the binding in mta.yaml:

modules:
  - name: your-application-name
    type: nodejs
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: ups-database-external
resources:
  - name: ups-database-external
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: user-provided
      service-name: ups-database-external

6: Manually Deploy the Database Objects

Since you are not using HDI, you need to manually deploy the database objects in your SAP HANA Cloud instance.

1. Generate the SQL Scripts

From your CDS models, generate the SQL scripts using the following commands:

cds compile db/ --to sql --src db cds compile srv/ --to sql --src srv

These commands generate SQL scripts from your model definitions in the db/ and srv/ directories.

2. Execute the Scripts in the Database Console

  • Access SAP HANA Database Explorer.
  • Connect to your SAP HANA Cloud instance.
  • Execute the generated SQL scripts to create the necessary tables and views in your schema.

Anthony Ramos Romero

entry.title: Using the new selective deletion functionality in SAP DSP to track record deletion in the source
entry.content:
With the latest update of SAP Datasphere, it is now possible to create selective deletions on your local tables created in SAP Datasphere. Obviously, this opens a whole new world of possibilities. One of them is to be able to handle deletions in the source, and even track them in your SAP Datasphere environment. In this series of blogs, I will explain how you could set up such a scenario. In this first blog I will explain how the new functionality for selective deletions on local tables in SAP Datasphere is used.

The new functionality to do selective deletions on your local tables is available from the Data Integration Monitor. From the Local Tables we are able to jump into the settings for a specific local table in SAP Datasphere:

We now get into this new screen:

We are quite easily able to either delete all the records, or selectively delete records from a table based on the value in a column. It is also possible to do this on the values of multiple columns, instead of just one, to really make the deletion specific.

When using the Filter option we have quite a few options to make the correct selection of data that has to be deleted from the local table in SAP Datasphere:

When we have selected the value that we want the deletion to happen on, we can click the Show Preview option to actually show what will be deleted.

This pops up a preview of the data that will be deleted. I expected that this would just be a subset of records, but SAP Datasphere is actually giving a preview of the data that will be deleted, which gives a nice confirmation of what is going to happen.

When clicking on the refresh button in the previous screen we get another confirmation of the number of records that is going to be deleted and the size of the records that will be deleted. With this you could get a nice estimate of the results of your action, and if you need to broaden or narrow your selection to free up more or less space.

Now when we press the button SAP Datasphere will start the deletion of our data based on the criteria.

In the Data Deletion Logs tab, we can keep track of the deletions that previously happened on this table.

We are also able to jump into more detail in what the deletion was by clicking the arrow in window button on the right. Unfortunately, at this moment in time it is not yet possible to see what the delete statement was based on, it will just show you the amount of records deleted.

 

Creating a Data Deletion Schedule

In the same way as manually deleting data you can also set-up a schedule to selectively delete data. We can create a schedule by following the wizard. The first step is to define a business name for the deletion schedule and give the filter values to delete on:

 

The second step is to define a schedule in the same way that we can define a schedule for data loading.

 

Finally in the last step we get an overview of what we scheduled. 

Unfortunately, there is no clear overview over the next deletion that will take place, which I think is something that would add value to have. We can however differentiate the scheduled deletions from the manual deletions because the object type is different:

Another thing that I really miss at this moment in time is the possibility to schedule the selective deletion in a Task Chain. For the use case that we are going to build in the next blog this would be awesome to have.

Conclusion

The new selective deletion functionality in SAP Datasphere provides a powerful way to manage data within local tables. It allows users to selectively remove records based on specific criteria, offering a preview feature to verify what will be deleted. While there are some limitations—such as the lack of a clear overview of scheduled deletions and the inability to add deletions to a Task Chain—this functionality still offers significant control and flexibility.

Particularly for scenarios where data is loaded from external sources like flat files with "full updates," this feature helps manage deletions effectively. By combining it with the power of joins, users can track new, existing, and deleted records, maintaining a clean and up-to-date data environment. Stay tuned for the next blog post, which will explore a practical example of how to leverage this new capability to its fullest potential.
